characters.name: Alborn
characters.description: AlbornBasic infoNicknames & aliasesThomas BrownHome planetMeridianOccupationLeader of Meridian ArmyVoice actorMark L. TaylorLove interestComicTVMiriadelMiriadelAppearanceHairBrownEyesBrownSpeciesMeridianiteRaceWhiteGenderMaleFamilyChildrenElyon Brown(Adopted daughter) Alborn(voiced by Mark L. Taylor) isElyon's adoptive father, a Meridianite who raised her inEarthusing the alias ofThomas Brownto hide her fromPhobos. In the comicsMeridian, he is head of the Meridian army and lives with Elyon in her castle. In the television series he became Head of the Royal Guard upon Elyon’s restoration in Season 2. In the comics, before they took Elyon away to Earth, he was commander of the Royal Guard.[citation needed]

characters.name: Alchemy
characters.description: AlchemyBasic infoHome planetEarthAge14-15Birthday1989/1990OccupationStudentVoice actorLauren TomAppearanceHairReddish-brownEyesGreenSpeciesHumanRaceWhiteGenderFemaleTV appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceHappy Birthday WillS is for Self Alchemy(voiced byLauren Tom[1]) is a minor character inseason 1and a recurring supporting characer inSeason 2of theW.I.T.C.H.TV series. Alchemy does not have a counterpart in the comics. Alchemy is one of the best friends ofElyonandCornelia, who is in the same classes with them, and seems to be the least feminine of them all; however, she is one of the most sensitive of the girls. Whenever she is upset, she gets cheered by listening to real depressing music. She has a small cat for a pet. Alchemy is one of the best friends ofElyonandCornelia, who is in the same classes with them, and seems to be the least feminine of them all; however, she is one of the most sensitive of the girls. Whenever she is upset, she gets cheered by listening to real depressing music. She has a small cat for a pet.

characters.name: Altermere Will
characters.description: Altermere WillBasic infoAge13  (Biologically)BirthdayUnknownOccupationStudentStatusSemi-aliveVoice actorKelly StablesAppearanceHairRedEyesBrownSpeciesAstral Drop(formally)AltermereRaceWhiteGenderFemaleMagicElementQuintessencePowersStandard Guardian AbilitiesElectrokinesisVitakinesisTV appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceH is for HuntedH is for Hunted Altermere Will, also known as "Clone Will" or "Alter Will", is a fictional character that appears in theTV series,W.I.T.C.H.This character is theAltermereof the leader of the W.I.T.C.H. and Quintessence guardianWill Vandom. Astral Will's appearance is identical  to that of the original Will's. Astral Will is a thirteen-year old eighth grader and has bright red, short hair in a bob cut. In addition her eyes are brown and big, and she also shares the same clothing as the real Will. Like the real Will, Astral Will is tomboyish, sensitive towards other people's feelings and has a fiery and unstable temper. Astral Will like the real Will, also had a crush onMatt Olsen. Astral Will also has issues with trusting people due to manipulation from Nerissa. When she was turned into anAltermereshe has a brighter personality and is noticeably friendlier and briefly lived Will's life with her friends and mother and enjoyed it so much that she attacked the real Will  when believing she wanted to reabsorb her in the Heart of Kandrakar, albeit it was only out of fear rather than malice. Despite only reconciling with Will only moments before, she sacrificed herself to save Will despite her own wish to live, which shows she is selfless.[1]

characters.name: Althor
characters.description: AlthorBasic infoHome planetKandrakarAppearanceHairWhiteEyesBlackSpeciesElfMagicElementFire Althoris an old man that bears some resemblance to an elf or a dwarf. Althor is a member of the congregation ofKandrakar. In the comics he is also established as a former guardian. At some point in the past, Althor was chosen to be aGuardianand eventually later joined the congregation of Kandrakar as one of its elders.

characters.name: Amanda Bolland
characters.description: Amanda BollandBasic infoHome planetEarthOccupationExecutive assistantVoice actorGrey DeLisleAppearanceHairBrownSpeciesHumanRaceWhiteGenderFemaleComic appearancesFirst appearanceIssue 014: The End of a DreamTV appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceE is for EnemyE is for Enemy Amanda Bollandis the executive assistant forSusan Vandom, at Simultech. In the television series she  is voiced by Grey DeLisle, who also voicesMiranda. When meeting W.I.T.C.H., Amanda tried very hard to get along with them, trying to use teen lingo in a way that makes her sound out of touch. She can ask a lot of pushy questions but seems to genuinely are about them too.[1]She is a very generous person, willing to lend money to who she thought wasWillwhen she wanted to go to the movies with her friends.[2] When meeting W.I.T.C.H., Amanda tried very hard to get along with them, trying to use teen lingo in a way that makes her sound out of touch. characters.name: Andrew Hornby
characters.description: Andrew HornbyBasic infoHome planetEarthOccupationStudentVoice actorBryne OffuttAppearanceHairBlond (comics)Black (TV series)EyesLight-greenSpeciesHumanRaceWhiteGenderMaleComic appearancesFirst appearanceIssue 002: The Twelve PortalsTV appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceL is for LoserT is for Trauma Andrew Hornby(voiced by Bryne Offutt[1]) is a recurring character in W.I.T.C.H. He appears in both thecomicsand Season 2 of theTV series, beingIrma Lair's not-so-secret crush. In the comics, Andrew was a popular boy in the ninth grade atSheffield Institute, noted for his blond hair. Not much was shown of his personality, but he did love beautiful older girls and acted a little too forward on a first meeting, only to be turned to a frog.[2] In the comics, Andrew was a popular boy in the ninth grade atSheffield Institute, noted for his blond hair. Not much was shown of his personality, but he did love beautiful older girls and acted a little too forward on a first meeting, only to be turned to a frog.[2] In the TV series, Andrew is a cool, popular senior with brown hair and a deep voice. Andrew has shown he cares about people respecting one another[3]and is against objectifying people.[4]Notably he also greatly enjoys dressing up for Halloween, though he told the whole school it was for babies to seem cool.[5]

characters.name: Anna Lair
characters.description: Anna LairBasic infoHome planetEarthVoice actorCandi MiloLove interestComicTVTom LairTom LairAppearanceHairBrownEyesBlueSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleFamilyChildrenIrma LairChristopher LairComic appearancesFirst appearanceIssue 002: The Twelve PortalsTV appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceHappy Birthday WillW is for Witch Christopher Lair Anna LairisIrma's stepmother in thecomicsand her biological mother in theTV series. Anna  is married toTom Lairand makes several appearances throughout the series. Her personality is similar to Irma's, but she is a little more mature. Anna is a little oblivious toChris's bad behavior, and seems to protect him from his sister.[citation needed]Irma has hinted that she gets embarrassed by Anna.[1][2]Anna likes to party, although in a "lame" way.[citation needed]Anna is proud of herhusbandbeing a police officer.[citation needed]She likes to go to the beach.[3][4]

characters.name: Annihilator
characters.description: AnnihilatorCommon powersLaser beamsHome planetMeridianAnnihilatorsare humanoid beings that appear in both the W.I.T.C.H.comicsandTV series. They are nearly unstoppable humanoid juggernauts, capable of causing mass destruction. They possess superhuman strength and have the ability to fire energy beams from their eyes at enemies.  In thecomics, the Annihilators appear to be tall humanoids that resemble theMeridianites(particularlyCedricandDaltar). They wear green hoods and light-greenish-brown robes. They have the symbol of the Meridian royal family or the Seal of Phobos on their hoods and belts.[1]

characters.name: Ardehl
characters.description: ArdehlBasic infoHome planetMeridianAppearanceHairWhiteSpeciesMeridianiteGenderMaleComic appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceSpecial Issue: Elyon: Return of the QueenSpecial Issue: Elyon: Return of the Queen Ardehlis an elderMeridianitewho made his sole appearance as a supporting role in the special issueElyon: Return of the Queen. WhenElyonandVathekwere captured byRascal's people, they were separated and Elyon was imprisoned with an old Meridianite - Ardehl. He explained that during the reign ofPhobos, people were forcefully enlisted into his army and that Ardehl led a small community to build an underground secret base where they were free from Phobos's tyrannical rule.[1]

characters.description: AriBasic infoHome planetArkhantaOccupationLord of ArkhantaLove interestComicJamayedaAppearanceHairBlueGenderMaleFamilyChildrenMaqiComic appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceIssue 028: So Close, Yet So Farunknown Ariis a main villain in theArc 3: A Crisis on Both Worldsof theW.I.T.C.H.comics. Ari is the Lord ofArkhantawho transformed the land by capturing thebansheeYua. He and his son Maqi later reappear as part of the supporting cast in later arcs.

characters.name: Arthur
characters.description: ArthurLove interestComicMareeveAppearanceHairBlondEyesBlueSpecieshumanRaceWhiteGenderMaleComic appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceIssue 113: The Long KissIssue 113: The Long Kiss Arthurwas the human man who loves starfish princessMareeve. Arthur is described as a very handsome man, compared to a Greek statute. He has a pretty muscular build with chin-length sandy-colored hair and blue eyes. Arthur is a loving man, caring deeply for Mareeve; he believed her story about being a deep-sea princess and accepted her as she was which Mareeve greatly appreciated. Arthur's most prominent trait is his loyalty as he is determined to remain with Mareeve despite the likelihood of her outliving him or having to return to her ocean home. When she has to move towns, he goes with her; and when she does return to the sea, he goes too despite the risks.

characters.name: As-Sharwa
characters.description: As-SharwaBasic infoHome planetKandrakarOccupationMember of the Council ofKandrakarAppearanceHairGreyGenderMaleComic appearancesFirst appearanceIssue 021: Under the Shadow Sign As-Sharwais one of the oldest and wisest of the inhabitants ofKandrakar,[citation needed]and the elected Voice of the Congregation.[citation needed] He has an elongated forehead tattooed with mystical runes, and long gray hair, along with many wrinkles. Below his chin is a device which might be a speech translator.

characters.name: Astral Cornelia
characters.description: Astral CorneliaBasic infoFull nameAstral CorneliaHome planetEarthAgeunknownBirthdayunknownStatusAliveLove interestComicRick FortworthAppearanceHairBlondeEyesIce blueSpeciesAstral Drop(Formerly: Comics)Human (Currently: Comics)GenderFemaleMagicPowersNoneComic appearancesFirst appearanceIssue 003: The Dark DimensionTV appearancesFirst appearanceAmbush At Torus Filney Astral Corneliais a fictional character that appears in thecomicsandTV series. This is theAstral Dropto theearthGuardianCornelia Hale. Originally Astral Cornelia was identical to her creator, a tall girl in her mid-teen with the same long blonde hair and ice blue eyes. Following the Astral Drops rebellion and fleeing, Astral Cornelia cut her hair short.

characters.name: Astral Drop
characters.description: Astral DropUnique featuresDuplicate of aGuardianHome planetEarth/Kandrakar AnAstral Dropis a magical doppelganger, summoned when theGuardianshave to go on a mission and need doubles so their absence is not felt. Although a Drop is a perfect physical copy of a person, in theTV series, they possess none of their memories, and have no personality of their own. In thecomics, they usually start as a prefect physical copy of a person with their memories and personality but can develop their own with time. In theTV series, an Astral Drop can become anAltermere. Yan LintoldW.I.T.C.H.about Astral Drops so that they could rescueCalebwhile the Astral Drops performed their play atschool. Unfortunately, the Astral Drops did not know the play.[1]

characters.name: Astral Will
characters.description: Astral WillBasic infoBirthdayUnknownOccupationStudentAppearanceHairRedEyesBrownSpeciesAstral Drop(formally)Human (Comics)RaceWhiteMagicPowersNone (Comic Series)Comic appearancesFirst appearanceThe Dark DimensionTV appearancesFirst appearanceAmbush at Torus Filney Astral Will, also known as "Alter Will", is a fictional character that appears in thecomicsandTV series,W.I.T.C.H.She is theAstral Dropof the leader of the W.I.T.C.H.,Will Vandom. Astral Will's appearance is originally exactly the same as the original Will. She is a fourteen-years old eighth grader and has bright red, short hair in a bob cut. Her eyes are brown and big. She also shares the same clothing as the real Will.

characters.name: Bess and Courtney Grumper
characters.description: Bess and Courtney GrumperComicsTV seriesBasic infoHome planetEarthOccupationStudentsVoice actorTracy Martin (Bess)Courtney Peldon (Courtney)AppearanceHairBlue (Comics)Red (TV)EyesGreenSpeciesHumanRaceWhiteGenderFemale Courtney Peldon (Courtney) Red (TV) The Grumper sisters love spreading gossip and find great fun in breaking up friendships.[1]They love to ridicule (especially when it comes to gossiping about others) anyone and get them in trouble at school to see their reactions, but theW.I.T.C.H. girls- and especiallyIrma LairandCornelia Hale- are their favorite targets.[2] In the comics, they are the writers of the gossip section of the Sheffield Institute newspaper, "Vanity Sheffield", though later are made the editors by Principal Knickerbocker.[2]In the TV series, they joined Irma's K-ship radio staff so that they can spread gossip across the school. They aren't above creating conflicts and tearing apart other's relationships in the name of gossip.[3] Bess and Courtney are big into fashions and rivals of Cornelia. They have been known to diss Cornelia and her fashion sense, such as when they were walking through an alley and dissing Cornelia.[4] They are considered to bePrincipal Knickerbocker's pets, though they don't mind bribing students to stand behind them.[5]Dean Collinsdeclared them the most "falsely respectful" students in the school. The two strive to look like model students, dedicated to their school work and never missing classes, even when sick. This has caused grief when they spread sickness to student and teachers alike.[2] They are considered rude by everyone except themselves due to their gossipy nature and are disliked by many students and especially by Irma because of the nasty rumors they make up about people. Even so, they are somewhat popular with boys on first meeting.[6]They have a positive relationship with Venus Sanders who, accepting their flattery, gives them free samples from her mother's cosmetics shop.[5]Their only other "friend" is Bertha Mills who follows their orders and circulates the newspaper for them. However, Bertha will gladly deflect blame to the sisters if it will keep her out of trouble or earn her points with her teachers.[2]

characters.name: Bitterroot
characters.description: BitterrootBasic infoHome planetZamballaLove interestTVIronwoodAppearanceSpeciesZamballanGenderMaleMagicPowersEnhanced strength and endurance, ability to create armored shell to protect himself during hibernationFamilyChildrenBowbreakerTV appearancesFirst appearanceLast appearanceP is for ProtectorsR is for Relentless BitterrootisIronwood's mate and also one of largest and most powerful Zamballans onZamballa. He shares his life on Zamballa with his wife Ironwood, and his child, Bowbreaker. At first, he was hostile towards theGuardianswhen he met them due to Nerissa manipulating him, but he eventually opened up to them and began to trust them.[citation needed] Bitterroot is a wise and protective father who cares for his wife and child dearly. He will do anything to protect them, even if it means he would never be able to see them again.[citation needed] Bitterroot is a wise and protective father who cares for his wife and child dearly. He will do anything to protect them, even if it means he would never be able to see them again.[citation needed]

characters.name: Bolgo
characters.description: BolgoBasic infoOccupationMember of the congregation ofKandrakarAppearanceHairWhiteGenderMaleComic appearancesFirst appearanceIssue 012: The Challenge of Phobos Bolgois a member of the Congregation ofKandrakarand is an elfin old man. Bolgo inquired ifthe OraclemeantKandrakarshould intervene during the final battle againstPhobos. Bolgo also discussed the Oracle's decision to simply wait and see "how events unfold" with several other Council members, includingLubaandAlthor.[1]
